q: Global warming refers to the gradual increase in the average temperature of the earth's ___.
Main greenhouse gases include carbon dioxide ($CO_2$), ___ ($CH_4$), and oxides of nitrogen ($NO_x$).
___ also destroy the earth's protective ozone layer which shields the earth from harmful ultraviolet (UV) rays.
Acid rains are caused mainly by the release of ___ and oxides of nitrogen.
Acidified clouds could travel great ___ before releasing the acid rain.
The oil industry adds ___ ($Pb$) to petrol to help engines run more smoothly.
Smoke from the combustion of leaded fuels causes cancer and high ___ pressure in adults.
Leaded fumes reduce intelligence in children, thus hindering ___ ability.
Excavated areas for coal mining that are not filled and revegetated cause ___ pollution.
Low frequency sound and ___ are usually beneath the threshold of human hearing.
Wind turbines may create ___ flicker on nearby residences.
Large dams are breeding grounds for ___ and cause the spread of disease.
Dams serve as a ___ sink, where water in dams is hotter than normal river water.
Geothermal power is a relatively ___ (less harmful) source of energy.
Biomass power plants require ___ for cooling, like coal and natural gas-fired power plants.
In wind energy transfer, ___ energy is converted to electrical energy.
For bio-fuel, chemical energy $\rightarrow$ thermal energy $\rightarrow$ ___ energy $\rightarrow$ electrical energy.
In hydroelectricity energy transfer, ___ energy is converted into kinetic energy.
